Node.description: Plugin to configure impairment and filter specifications
Node.help: Impairment plugin configures interfaces on the Port CPU to impair selected outgoing traffic. Impairment configuration specifies the type of impairment and the filter configuration specifies the selection criteria.

PropertyBoolean.name: addDelay
PropertyBoolean.description: This flag must be set to add latency

PropertyBoolean.name: addReorder
PropertyBoolean.description: This flag must be set to add packet reordering

PropertyBoolean.name: addDrop
PropertyBoolean.description: This flag must be set to add packet loss

PropertyBoolean.name: addDuplicate
PropertyBoolean.description: This flag must be set to add packet duplication

PropertyBoolean.name: addBandwidth
PropertyBoolean.description: This flag must be set to limit the rate of traffic

PropertyBoolean.name: randomizeSeed
PropertyBoolean.description: Select this to randomize seed for impairment

PropertyInt.name: delay
PropertyInt.description: Delay(ms) to be added to each packet

PropertyInt.name: reorder
PropertyInt.description: Delay(ms) to be added to each packet selected to be re-ordered

PropertyInt.name: reorderLength
PropertyInt.description: Number of consecutive packets to re-order.

PropertyString.name: destinationIp
PropertyString.description: IP address or subnet. Impairment is applied to only those packets destined to this address/subnet

PropertyInt.name: sourcePort
PropertyInt.description: Source port number. Impairment is applied to only those packets which have this source port number

PropertyInt.name: destinationPort
PropertyInt.description: Destination port number. Impairment is applied to only those packets which have this destination port number

PropertyString.name: protocol
PropertyString.description: Protocol name. Impairment is applied to only those packets which use this protocol.

PropertyString.name: typeOfService
PropertyString.description: Type of service bits. Impairment is applied to only those packets which belong to this TOS

PropertyString.name: bandwidthUnits
PropertyString.description: Bandwidth unit used for traffic limitation.

PropertyInt.name: drop
PropertyInt.description: Percentage of packets to be dropped.

PropertyInt.name: duplicate
PropertyInt.description: Percentage of packets to be duplicated

PropertyInt.name: jitter
PropertyInt.description: Jitter(ms) specifies the amount of variation in delay

PropertyInt.name: gap
PropertyInt.description: Specifies the number of packets to pass through before reordering a packet

PropertyInt.name: bandwidth
PropertyInt.description: Rate(kBps). Specifies the maximum allowable rate per interface

PropertyBoolean.obsolete: global is obsoleted, please use impairGlobal instead
PropertyBoolean.name: global
PropertyBoolean.description: This is set to add impairment to every interface in the underlying ethernet layer bound to an IP address existing in the IP layer above the impairment layer

PropertyBoolean.name: impairGlobal
PropertyBoolean.description: This is set to add impairment to every interface in the underlying ethernet layer bound to an IP address existing in the IP layer above the impairment layer

PropertyBoolean.name: addDropSequence
PropertyBoolean.description: This flag must be set to add drop packet sequence

PropertyInt.name: dropSequenceSkip
PropertyInt.description: Number of packets to leave unaffected between drop operations.

PropertyInt.name: dropSequenceLength
PropertyInt.description: Number of consecutive packets to drop.

PropertyBoolean.name: addReorderPI
PropertyBoolean.description: This flag must be set to add fixed packet interval delay.

PropertyInt.name: reorderPISkip
PropertyInt.description: Number of packets to leave unaffected between re-order operations.

PropertyInt.name: reorderPILength
PropertyInt.description: Number of consecutive packets to re-order.

PropertyInt.name: reorderPIInterval
PropertyInt.description: Number of packets to skip until delayed packets to be transmited.

PropertyInt.name: reorderPITimeout
PropertyInt.description: Maximum time to keep packets delayed.

PropertyStringList.name: operationOrder
PropertyStringList.description: The order in which the impairment operations will be applied. Each item represents the name of an operation. The first item represents the first operation to be applied. The default operation order is: kDelay, kDrop, kDropSequence, kReorder, kReorderPI, kDuplicate, kBandwidth.
